title: Remedies Against the Last Deceptions
subtitle: Ludolph teaches the soul how to stand against false visions before the coming of the Lord

stillness: Be still, and do not reach upward in pride.
Ask for nothing above your nature; only sink into your own nothingness.
Wait here in great humility, in great fear, in reverence before God.
gospel: Before the coming of the Lord,
many false christs and false prophets shall arise —
who before seemed even to be perfect.
behold: See now the snares the devil scatters in these last days,
in the hearts of those who wish to give themselves to God
by prayer and contemplation, and by other works of perfection.
He deceives the soul in the very rule it should keep toward God,
and in that which belongs to God.
He hides falsity beneath the appearance of truth,
and malice beneath the likeness of goodness,
that he may the more often spread mortal error.
O how many souls have been deceived in this way!
consider: Do not desire visions or revelations, or feelings above nature.
Such desire cannot be without a root of pride and presumption,
without some vain curiosity about the secrets of God,
or without frailty and a failing of faith.
For these, the justice of God abandons the soul that desires such things,
and at times it falls into the temptation and illusion of the devil.
True revelations do not come through your desire,
nor through any striving or study the soul has in itself;
they come only through the pure goodness of God,
into a soul that dwells in great humility and great fear.
In your prayer, hold to no consolation
founded on presumption and the proud esteem of yourself,
which leads you into ambition for your own honor and glory.
Bear only that consolation which comes from a complete sense
of your own nothingness and imperfection,
and from the greatness and height of God, held in deep reverence.
Whatever feeling, however lofty, whatever vision, however secret,
if it draws your heart against any article of faith,
or against good morals, and above all against humility —
flee it and shudder at it; without doubt it comes from the devil.
And if a vision appears of which you are certain it comes from God,
do not lean upon it, nor rest yourself in it.
Flee the friendship of those who sow and spread these temptations,
and of those who uphold and praise them;
do not listen to their words, nor wish to see their ways.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, meek and humble of heart,
ground my heart in true humility,
and shut out from me every motion of presumption and pride,
that I may never desire visions or revelations,
nor be led astray by any spiritual temptations
that could in any way draw me into error.
Make me, most loving Lord, to rise each day to the works of the virtues,
and ever to advance in good,
and continually to tend and sigh toward a higher life;
that thus, by watching and acting carefully,
I may be able to prepare myself for Your uncertain coming. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Verae revelationes, et sentimenta spiritualia secretorum Dei, non veniunt per desiderium, neque per aliquod conamen vel studium quod anima habeat in se; sed tantum veniunt per puram bonitatem Dei in animam, quae est in magna humilitate, et in magno timore, ac reverentia Dei.
latinJewel.english: True revelations, and spiritual feelings of the secrets of God, do not come through desire, nor through any striving or study the soul has in itself; but they come only through the pure goodness of God into a soul that dwells in great humility, and in great fear, and reverence of God.
